quotes.text: A beginning is the time for taking the most delicate care that the balances are correct.
Arrakis teaches the attitude of the knife — chopping off what's incomplete and saying: 'Now it's complete because it's ended here.'

quotes.source: from a Reverend Mother to Muad'Dib
quotes.text: The mystery of life isn't a problem to solve, but a reality to experience.
There is no escape — we pay for the violence of our ancestors.
It is said in the desert that possession of water in great amount can inflict a man with fatal carelessness.
Many have marked the speed with which Muad'Dib learned the necessities of Arrakis. Muad'Dib learned rapidly because his first training was in how to learn.
Greatness is a transitory experience. It is never consistent. It depends in part upon the myth-making imagination of humankind.
Without change, something sleeps inside us, and seldom awakens. The sleeper must awaken.
Deep in the human unconscious is a pervasive need for a logical universe that makes sense. But the real universe is always one step beyond logic.
Once men turned their thinking over to machines in the hope that this would set them free. But that only permitted other men with machines to enslave them.
When religion and politics travel in the same cart, the riders believe nothing can stand in their way.
The convoluted wording of legalisms grew up around the necessity to hide from ourselves the violence we intend toward one another.
How often it is that the angry man rages denial of what his inner self is telling him.
There exists no separation between gods and men; one blends softly casual into the other.
Truth is a chameleon.
The willow submits to the wind and prospers until one day it is many willows — a wall against the wind. This is the willow's purpose.
When I need to identify rebels, I look for men with principles.
Any path that narrows future possibilities may become a lethal trap.
Education is no substitute for intelligence.
To know a thing well, know its limits. Only when pushed beyond its tolerances will its true nature be seen.
Government is a shared myth. When the myth dies, the government dies.

Most discipline is hidden discipline, designed not to liberate but to limit. Do not ask 'Why?' Be cautious of 'How?' 'Why?' leads inexorably to paradox. 'How?' traps you in a universe of cause and effect.
Memory never recaptures reality. Memory reconstructs. All reconstructions change the original.
Absolute power does not corrupt absolutely, absolute power attracts the corruptible.
The mind imposes this framework which it calls 'reality.' That arbitrary framework has a tendency to be quite independent of what your senses report.
When I am weaker than you, I ask you for freedom because that is according to your principles; when I am stronger than you, I take away your freedom because that is according to my principles.
Most civilization is based on cowardice. It's so easy to civilize by teaching cowardice. You water down the standards which would lead to bravery.
Bureaucracy destroys initiative. There is little that bureaucrats hate more than innovation, especially innovation that produces better results than the old routines.
The function of armies is to demonstrate the failures of statecraft.
Enemies strengthen you. Allies weaken.
All proofs inevitably lead to propositions which have no proof! All things are known because we want to believe in them.
Ignorance is the parent of fear.
The capacity to learn is a gift; the ability to learn is a skill; the willingness to learn is a choice.
It is the fate of single-minded people that, even when they hit the target, they miss everything else.
Power attracts the corruptible. Suspect any who seek it.
There is no real ending. It's just the place where you stop the story.
Show me a completely smooth operation and I'll show you someone who's covering mistakes. Real boats rock.
Every judgment teeters on the brink of error. To claim absolute knowledge is to become monstrous. Knowledge is an unending adventure at the edge of uncertainty.
When you think to take another's life, remember that your own is included.
What do you despise? By this are you truly known.
Survival is the ability to swim in strange water.
Truth suffers from too much analysis.
Hope clouds observation.

I must not fear. Fear is the mind-killer. Fear is the little-death that brings total obliteration. I will face my fear. I will permit it to pass over me and through me. And when it has gone past I will turn the inner eye to see its path. Where the fear has gone there will be nothing. Only I will remain.
A process cannot be understood by stopping it. Understanding must move with the flow of the process, must join it and flow with it.
The mind commands the body and it obeys. The mind orders itself and meets resistance.
The Fremen were supreme in that quality the ancients called 'spannungsbogen' — the self-imposed delay between desire for a thing and the act of reaching out to grasp it.
God created Arrakis to train the faithful.
Polish comes from the cities; wisdom from the desert.
He who can destroy a thing, controls a thing.
Small souls who seek power over others first destroy the faith those others might have in themselves.
You cannot manipulate a marionette with only one string.

quotes.source: Manual of Muad'Dib by Princess Irulan
quotes.text: Muad'Dib could indeed see the Future, but you must understand the limits of this power. Think of sight. You have eyes, yet cannot see without light. If you are on the floor of a valley, you cannot see beyond your valley. Just so, Muad'Dib could not always choose to look across the mysterious terrain. He tells us that a single obscure decision of prophecy, perhaps the choice of one word over another, could change the entire aspect of the future. He tells us 'The vision of time is broad, but when you pass through it, time becomes a narrow door.' And always, he fought the temptation to choose a clear, safe course, warning 'That path leads ever down into stagnation.'
The slow blade penetrates the shield.
Knowing where the trap is — that's the first step in evading it.
The proximity of a desirable thing tempts one to overindulgence.
People need hard times to develop psychic muscles.
Observe the plans within plans within plans.
The purpose of argument is to change the nature of truth.
Respect for the truth comes close to being the basis for all morality.
A popular man arouses the jealousy of the powerful.
Parting with friends is a sadness. A place is only a place.
In politics, the tripod is the most unstable of all structures.
If wishes were fishes, we'd all cast nets.
Let us not rail about justice as long as we have arms and the freedom to use them.
I must rule with eye and claw — as the hawk among lesser birds.
Be prepared to appreciate what you meet.
The highest function of ecology is understanding consequences.
Science is made up of so many things that appear obvious after they are explained.
To save one from a mistake is a gift of paradise.
Belief can be manipulated. Only knowledge is dangerous.
The difference between a good administrator and a bad one is about five heartbeats.
Laws to suppress tend to strengthen what they would prohibit.
Seek freedom and become captive of your desires. Seek discipline and find your liberty.

quotes.source: Muad'Dib: Family Commentaries by Princess Irulan
quotes.text: Any road followed precisely to its end leads precisely nowhere.
No more terrible disaster could befall your people than for them to fall into the hands of a Hero.

quotes.source: Muad'Dib: The Ninety-nine Wonders of The Universe by Princess Irulan
quotes.text: When law and duty are one, united by religion, you never become fully conscious.
The concept of progress acts as a protective mechanism to shield us from the terrors of the future.
Beyond a critical point within a finite space, freedom diminishes as numbers increase.
The thing the ecologically illiterate don't realize about an ecosystem is that it's a system.
Every civilization must contend with an unconscious force which can block, betray or countermand almost any conscious intention.
Do not be trapped by the need to achieve anything. This way, you achieve everything.
If you need something to worship, then worship life — all life, every last crawling bit of it!
Here lies a toppled god — His fall was not a small one. We did but build his pedestal, a narrow and a tall one.
Often I must speak other than I think. That is called diplomacy.

quotes.source: The Apocrypha of Muad'Dib, Children of Dune
quotes.text: Atrocity is recognized as such by victim and perpetrator alike. Atrocity merely arms the future for more atrocity.

quotes.source: The Preacher at Arrakeen, Children of Dune
quotes.text: This is the fallacy of power: ultimately it is effective only in an absolute, a limited universe.

quotes.source: The Spacing Guild Manual, Children of Dune
quotes.text: Good government never depends upon laws, but upon the personal qualities of those who govern.

quotes.source: Politics as Repeat Phenomenon: Bene Gesserit Training Manual, Children of Dune
quotes.text: Governments, if they endure, always tend increasingly toward aristocratic forms.

quotes.source: Inscription on the storehouse at Dar-es-Balat, God Emperor of Dune
quotes.text: I have come to believe that holy boredom is good and sufficient reason for the invention of free will.

quotes.source: Hwi Noree, God Emperor of Dune
quotes.text: You know it's love when you want to give joy and damn the consequences.
Power bases are very dangerous because they attract people who are truly insane, people who seek power only for the sake of power.

quotes.source: Muad'Dib, From the Oral History (God Emperor of Dune)
quotes.text: The problem of leadership is inevitably: Who will play God?
Governments can be useful to the governed only so long as inherent tendencies toward tyranny are restrained.

quotes.source: Leto Atreides II, God Emperor of Dune
quotes.text: It is difficult to live in the present, pointless to live in the future and impossible to live in the past.

quotes.source: Duncan Idaho recites an ancient Fremen saying, God Emperor of Dune
quotes.text: You should never be in the company of anyone with whom you would not want to die.

quotes.source: Reverend Mother Taraza, Heretics of Dune
quotes.text: In my estimation, more misery has been created by reformers than by any other force in human history. Show me someone who says, 'Something must be done!' and I will show you a head full of vicious intentions that have no other outlet.

quotes.source: Bashar Miles Teg, Heretics of Dune
quotes.text: You could drag humankind almost anywhere by manipulating the enormous energies of procreation. Bottle it up and it becomes monstrously dangerous. Redirect it and it will sweep over anything in its path. This is an ultimate secret of all religions.

quotes.source: Mother Superior Darwi Odrade, Chapterhouse: Dune
quotes.text: Live the best life you can. Life is a game whose rules you learn if you leap into it and play it to the hilt.
The person who takes the banal and ordinary and illuminates it in a new way can terrify. We do not want our ideas changed.
Ready comprehension is often a knee-jerk response and the most dangerous form of understanding. All comprehension is temporary.

quotes.source: Mother Superior Darwi Odrade, Chapterhouse: Dune
quotes.text: Give me the judgment of balanced minds in preference to laws every time. Codes and manuals create patterned behavior. All patterned behavior tends to go unquestioned, gathering destructive momentum.

quotes.source: Mother Superior Darwi Odrade, Chapterhouse: Dune
quotes.text: Intelligence takes chance with limited data in an arena where mistakes are not only possible but also necessary.
Ultimately all things are known because you want to believe you know.
The mind can go either direction under stress — toward positive or toward negative: on or off.
Power tends to isolate those who hold too much of it. Eventually, they lose touch with reality... and fall.
Was it defeat to choose a lesser evil?
You do not take from this universe. It grants you what it will.
There exists a limit to the force even the most powerful may apply without destroying themselves.
I give you the desert chameleon, whose ability to blend itself into the background tells you all you need to know about the roots of ecology and the foundations of a personal identity.
Atrocity never balances or rectifies the past. Atrocity merely arms the future for more atrocity.
Above all else, the mentat must be a generalist, not a specialist.
If you believe certain words, you believe their hidden arguments.
Peace demands solutions, but we never reach living solutions; we only work toward them.
The trouble with peace is that it tends to punish mistakes instead of rewarding brilliance.
The one-eyed view of our universe says you must not look far afield for problems.
Often there's no need to tear off an arm to remove a splinter.
To stay awake all night adds a day to your life.
Things we do without thinking — there's the real danger.
Paradox is a pointer telling you to look beyond it.
How persistent it is, this demand that our gods be perfect.
When strangers meet, great allowances should be made for differences of custom and training.
Some people never observe anything. Life just happens to them.
While you cause a granular universe to persist in your awareness, you are blind to movement.
When things change, your absolute universe vanishes.
Confine yourself to observing and you always miss the point of your own life.
There's no secret to balance. You just have to feel the waves.
All states are abstractions.
No sweeteners will cloak some forms of bitterness. If it tastes bitter, spit it out.

quotes.source: Appendix II: The Religion of Dune
quotes.text: Scientists seek the lawfulness of events. It is the task of Religion to fit man into this lawfulness.

quotes.source: Appendix II: The Religion of Dune
quotes.text: All men must see that the teaching of religion by rules and rote is largely a hoax. The proper teaching is recognized with ease. You can know it without fail because it awakens within you that sensation which tells you this is something you've always known.

quotes.source: Appendix II: The Religion of Dune
quotes.text: Religion must remain an outlet for people who say to themselves, 'I am not the kind of person I want to be.' It must never sink into an assemblage of the self-satisfied.

Experts and specialists lead you quickly into chaos. They are a source of useless nit-picking, the ferocious quibble over a comma. The mentat-generalist, on the other hand, should bring to decision-making a healthy common sense. He must not cut himself off from the broad sweep of what is happening in his universe. He must remain capable of saying: 'There's no real mystery about this at the moment. This is what we want now. It may prove wrong later, but we'll correct that when we come to it.' The mentat-generalist must understand that anything which we can identify as our universe is merely a part of larger phenomena. But the expert looks backward; he looks into the narrow standards of his own specialty. The generalist looks outward; he looks for living principles, knowing full well that such principles change, that they develop. It is to the characteristics of change itself that the mentat-generalist must look. There can be no permanent catalogue of such change, no handbook or manual. You must look at it with as few preconceptions as possible, asking yourself: 'Now what is this thing doing?'
There is probably no more terrible instant of enlightenment than the one in which you discover your father is a man — with human flesh.
There should be a science of discontent. People need hard times and oppression to develop psychic muscles.

There is in all things a pattern that is part of our universe. It has symmetry, elegance, and grace — those qualities you find always in that which the true artist captures. You can find it in the turning of the seasons, in the way sand trails along a ridge, in the branch clusters of the creosote bush or the pattern of its leaves. We try to copy these patterns in our lives and our society, seeking the rhythms, the dances, the forms that comfort. Yet, it is possible to see peril in the finding of ultimate perfection. It is clear that the ultimate pattern contains its own fixity. In such perfection, all things move toward death.

To attempt an understanding of Muad'Dib without understanding his mortal enemies, the Harkonnens, is to attempt seeing Truth without knowing Falsehood. It is the attempt to see the Light without knowing Darkness. It cannot be.

Thus spoke St. Alia-of-the-Knife: 'The Reverend Mother must combine the seductive wiles of a courtesan with the untouchable majesty of a virgin goddess, holding these attributes in tension so long as the powers of her youth endure. For when youth and beauty have gone, she will find that the place-between, once occupied by tension, has become a wellspring of cunning and resourcefulness.'

You have read that Muad'Dib had no playmates his own age on Caladan. The dangers were too great. But Muad'Dib did have wonderful companion-teachers. There was Gurney Halleck, the troubadour-warrior. You will sing some of Gurney's songs as you read along in this book. There was Thufir Hawat, the old Mentat Master of Assassins, who struck fear even into the heart of the Padishah Emperor. There were Duncan Idaho, the Swordmaster of the Ginaz; Dr. Wellington Yueh, a name black in treachery but bright in knowledge; the Lady Jessica, who guided her son in the Bene Gesserit Way, and — of course — the Duke Leto, whose qualities as a father have long been overlooked.

YUEH (yu'e), Wellington (weling-tun), Stdrd 10,082–10,191; medical doctor of the Suk School (grd Stdrd 10,112); md: Wanna Marcus, B.G. (Stdrd 10,092–10,186?); chiefly noted as betrayer of Duke Leto Atreides. (Cf: Bibliography, Appendix VII [Imperial conditioning] and Betrayal, The.)

How do we approach the study of Muad'Dib's father? A man of surpassing warmth and surprising coldness was the Duke Leto Atreides. Yet, many facts open the way to this Duke: his abiding love for his Bene Gesserit lady; the dreams he held for his son; the devotion with which men served him. You see him there — a man snared by Destiny, a lonely figure with his light dimmed behind the glory of his son. Still, one must ask: What is the son but an extension of the father?

With the Lady Jessica and Arrakis, the Bene Gesserit system of sowing implant-legends through the Missionaria Protectiva came to its full fruition. The wisdom of seeding the known universe with a prophecy pattern for the protection of B.G. personnel has long been appreciated, but never have we seen a condition-utextremis with more ideal mating of person and preparation. The prophetic legends had taken on Arrakis even to the extent of adopted labels (including Reverend Mother, canto and respondu, and most of the Shari-a panoplia propheticus). And it is generally accepted now that the Lady Jessica's latent abilities were grossly underestimated.
'Yueh! Yueh! Yueh!' goes the refrain. 'A million deaths were not enough for Yueh!'
